# Requirements for Adding, Changing, or Removing a Feature

* Complete the template below. Pull requests without enough context to review may be closed until the required information is provided.
* This template is only for functionality changes that have already been discussed with the maintainers.
* Update the test suite to cover the changed functionality when appropriate. For help, see <https://pester.dev/docs/quick-start>.
* All status checks must pass before a maintainer reviews the pull request. See [.github/CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/Sofiane-77/PSChristmasTree/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests) for the current workflow.

## Maintainer-Endorsed Discussion

<!--

Link the GitHub Discussion related to this change. New features and enhancements must be discussed and acknowledged by the maintainers before a pull request is submitted.

If your idea has not been discussed yet, open a discussion in the Ideas & Suggestions category first:
[Ideas & Suggestions](https://github.com/Sofiane-77/PSChristmasTree/discussions/categories/ideas-suggestions)

For other types of changes, use the appropriate template from [.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE](https://github.com/Sofiane-77/PSChristmasTree/tree/main/.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE).

-->
```none
```

-->

<!-- What are the possible side-effects or negative impacts of the code change? -->

## Verification Process

<!--

What process did you follow to verify that your change has the desired effects?

- How did you verify that all new functionality works as expected?
- How did you verify that all changed functionality works as expected?
- How did you verify that the change has not introduced any regressions?

Describe the actions you performed (including text you typed, commands you ran, etc.), and describe the results you observed.

-->
```none
```

## Release Notes

<!--

Please describe the changes in a single line that explains this improvement in
terms that a user can understand. This text will be used in PSChristmasTree's release notes.

If this change is not user-facing or notable enough to be included in release notes
you may use the strings "Not applicable" or "N/A" here.

-->
